sim.title: Claude — case study draft
sim.label: Claude
sim.prompt: Turn the Harbor House install into a one-page case study. Problem, what we wired in, what changed after 30 days. Keep it honest.
sim.response:
Case Study — Harbor House Inn (illustrative)

Problem: evening calls went to voicemail. The desk was catching 6 of 10.

The install: missed call → transcript → drafted reply → booking logged. Live in an afternoon.

30 days in: recovered bookings the desk never touched, and the owner's line: "it pays for itself the first weekend."

Next door, the restaurant wants the same intake fix.

reveals.title: Package one named problem
reveals.body: Fixed scope: $2–5K, one deadline

reveals.title: Three failure modes
reveals.body: Revenue stops when you stop · 3–5 clients = concentration risk · Every install is yours to maintain

asset.title: Three failure modes
bullets: Every install is yours to maintain

risk.title: Revenue stops when you stop.
risk.body: This is a service business. Retainers soften that; they don't cure it. At 3–5 clients, losing one is losing 25 % of your income.
bullets: You are the delivery capacity — vacations cost real dollars
Every install is yours to maintain until you hand it off

reveals.title: The real output
reveals.body: Income that isn't a paycheck · Compounding skill stack · Client list = distribution

caseFile.workflow: Every install = one more workflow you own + one warm client on the ledger
caseFile.result: Income + skill + distribution that all compound
